page: Vultr vc2-6c-16gb is an x86_64 architecture server within the Cloud Compute family. It features 6 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, with 6 physical cores and 1.0 thread per core. The processor includes 32 KB L1d cache, 4 MB L2 cache, and 16 MB L3 cache. It supports hardware instruction sets such as AVX, AVX-512F, AES, BMI2, and FMA.

The server is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 2.67 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled and consists of a 320 GB NVMe SSD. Network connectivity includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU hardware is present on this instance.

Benchmark data indicates a divergence in performance capabilities. In single-core stress-ng CPU testing, the instance scores 1272.09, placing it in the weak category. However, multi-core stress-ng CPU performance scales to 6774.42, which is categorized as average. Because it utilizes a shared CPU allocation, it offers a cost-efficient profile for workloads that can leverage multiple cores simultaneously without requiring sustained, dedicated single-core performance.

This resource configuration is suitable for multi-threaded applications, general-purpose web serving, development environments, and lightweight database tasks that benefit from NVMe storage but do not demand continuous, dedicated CPU execution.
description: The Vultr vc2-6c-16gb is an x86_64 Cloud Compute server featuring 6 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs clocked at 2.0 GHz, 16.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 320 GB NVMe SSD. Its CPU architecture supports AVX, AVX-512F, and AES instructions. In performance testing, the server exhibits a weak single-core stress-ng CPU score of 1272.09, but achieves an average multi-core stress-ng CPU score of 6774.42. This performance profile, combined with its shared CPU allocation, makes it a cost-effective option for multi-threaded workloads that do not require dedicated CPU resources. It is well-suited for general-purpose web serving, development environments, and lightweight database applications.
